name: Kiriboshi (Chicken Simmered with White Radish
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Chicken breast, boned, unskinned, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
PREPARE AHEAD:
Wash the daikon thoroughly under cold running water. Cover with 2 quarts of cold water in a bowl and refrigerate for at least 8 hours or overnight. Drain the daikon and transfer it to a 3-quart pot. Cover with 2 quarts of cold water and bring to a boil over high heat. Lower the heat to moderate and cook the daikon uncovered for 2 hours, or until it offers no resistance when pierced with the tip of a sharp knife. Drain the daikon in a sieve.
TO COOK:
Combine the dashi, sugar, salt, MSG, soy sauce, and sake in a 2-quart saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat. Add the chicken and daikon, and reduce the heat to its lowest point. Set a cover smaller than the pan directly on the food and simmer undisturbed for 1 1/2 hours. Serve as part of a Japanese meal or in larger portions as a luncheon dish.
